title: Object-Format Transition, Compatibility, and Disaster Defense · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, release, and operations council. The council needs measured maintenance and backup, fresh restore proof, Git 3.0 and SHA-256 readiness, damaged-mirror recovery, policy owners, drills, residual risks, and an accessible handoff. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Repository Migration and Disaster Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Case G67 is a project in a disposable repository. the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs. The learner must predict format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats, run git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output, explain storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract, test this changed condition: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-production-repository-defense-1j1x39" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Production Repository Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-production-repository-defense-1j1x39
# scenario-key: trace-1j1x39 probe-93x1j1 boundary-1x391j owner-1j1x39x fault-1j1x39z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1j candidate=b00b139
# hypothesis: the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs
git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output
# expected: format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats
# observation: storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract
# changed-case: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain
# after-ref: main=a11ce1j candidate=c0ffee9
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting
# transfer-limit: object-format migration and Git 3.0 rollout require current hosting and tool compatibility trials
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Production Repository Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Gita Production Repository Defense output and hide the violated Git 3.0 readiness and disaster-recovery defense assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Production Repository Defense without its stated preconditions, omit the Git 3.0 readiness and disaster-recovery defense trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Production Repository Defense for Git 3.0 readiness and disaster-recovery defense.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Gita Production Repository Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G76 is a project in a disposable repository. the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs. The learner must predict format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats, run git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output, explain storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract, test this changed condition: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting. Competency probe: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Production Repository Defense through the concrete Git 3.0 readiness and disaster-recovery defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.

hints: Name the Gita Production Repository Defense precondition or invariant that the project case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G76 is a project in a disposable repository. the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs. The learner must predict format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats, run git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output, explain storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract, test this changed condition: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting. Competency probe: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successful restore drill proves future incidents will have identical data, access, timing, and failure modes.
